title: Altenglische Legenden: Kindheit Jesu; Geburt Jesu; Barlaam und Josaphat; St. Patrik’s Fegefeuer (1875 compilation)
summary: The repository artifact is a pinned copy of University of Michigan digital item AFY7823.0001.001, a 2006 uncorrected OCR/TEI Level 1 encoding derived from Carl Horstmann's 1875 Altenglische Legenden. The encoded unit retains the title page, represents forty-two omitted preliminary pages as empty PB milestones, and encodes the six printed divisions on pp. 1-240. Those divisions contain eight non-overlapping intellectual texts through ten full or deliberately incomplete manuscript streams: Laud Infancy; Ashmole and Egerton Conception versions followed by the distinct Egerton Expanded Nativity; Bodley Barlaam; three Patrick/Purgatory versions; Vernon Jesus and the Masters; Vernon Barlaam; and Harley 4196 Barlaam. Horstmann also collated Cotton Julius D IX and Bodley 779 selectively for Patrick and discussed Harley 2277 although its opening Patrick leaves were already lost. Four complete Digital Bodleian objects permit direct boundary verification; no complete current public images were found for the British Library witnesses. A complete Internet Archive/Google scan verifies the edition, while U-M's historically described 284 TIFF production images are not currently retrievable through the tested routes.
review_notes: The cataloging subject is Horstmann's exact edition-level compilation, not one medieval work and not the complete forty-four-page introduction.
The Bodley Barlaam locator ff. 288v-302r is a direct-image correction to the current catalog and DIMEV claim ff. 228v-302r; both claims retain explicit provenance.
DIMEV 5875 overlaps the f. 39v portion of DIMEV 938 and is not a ninth non-overlapping work.
Rights records distinguish medieval wording, 1875 editorial matter, U-M encoding, modern catalog data, Digital Bodleian photography, and scan-provider terms.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for afy7823
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AFY7823.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 uncorrected OCR text encoded at Level 1 under the TEI in Libraries guidelines. It retains the edition title page, replaces forty-two preliminary pages with empty PB milestones, and serializes interleaved physical edition pages into reading-order XML.

entities.label: Historical U-M page-image set for AFY7823.0001.001
entities.description: The U-M production layer linked from the 2006 OCR. The XML header describes 284 600 dpi TIFF G4 page images, and its PB elements preserve a complete unique filename run 00000001.tif-00000284.tif. REF and SEQ diverge when interleaved physical edition pages are serialized into electronic reading order. No current public image endpoint or complete retrievable bundle was verified.
entities.reproduction_format: Historically described as 284 600 dpi TIFF G4 page images
notes: All 284 REF filenames and all 284 SEQ values are unique and consecutive; only 234 numeric REF values equal their corresponding SEQ because Ashmole/Egerton and Egerton/Laud physical page streams are serialized by reading order.
Unknown survival status records that current retrievability of the TIFF set has not been established; it does not imply that U-M destroyed the source files.

entities.label: Carl Horstmann, Altenglische Legenden (1875)
entities.description: The immediate printed source of the U-M encoding and the cataloging boundary: a German introduction of pp. i-xliv followed by six editorial divisions on pp. 1-240, printing ten complete or deliberately incomplete witness streams and selected variant apparatus.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, ed., Altenglische Legenden: Kindheit Jesu; Geburt Jesu; Barlaam und Josaphat; St. Patrik’s Fegefeuer (Paderborn: Ferdinand Schöningh, 1875).
bibliographic.title: Altenglische Legenden: Kindheit Jesu; Geburt Jesu; Barlaam und Josaphat; St. Patrik’s Fegefeuer

entities.label: Internet Archive delivery of the Google/Harvard scan of Horstmann 1875
entities.description: A complete 304-page scan of Harvard University's copy, digitized by Google and delivered by Internet Archive with OCR, scandata, and item metadata. The cataloged content appears on PDF page 11 and PDF pages 55-294.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page-image scan with plain-text OCR and scan metadata

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 108
entities.description: Direct source of the Infancy poem at ff. 11ra-22rb and the Patrick/Purgatory version at ff. 96v-104r. The latter omits the opening life and begins with Purgatory.

date_statements.label: Target hand and early core
date_statements.value: late thirteenth or early fourteenth century

notes: The codex has later additions; both target passages belong to the early Hand A core.
The catalog localizes Hand A's scribal dialect to West Oxfordshire and describes an uncertain possible Oxford origin; neither is a proven composition place.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 43
entities.description: Horstmann's 632-line Patrick base witness at ff. 34r-41v and his 278-line Conception of Mary version at ff. 208v-212r.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: first quarter of the fourteenth century

notes: The catalog localizes the scribe's orthography to Gloucestershire, eLALME LP 7170; this is witness evidence, not a composition-place assertion.

entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 1993
entities.description: Direct source of the continuous Zur Geburt Jesu stream at ff. 27-40 and the complete Patrick stream at ff. 119v-128r. The first 276 encoded birth lines are DIMEV 4178; the following 897 are DIMEV 938.

notes: The public catalog uses a historical description and supplies no current complete digital object.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodley 779
entities.description: Direct source of the 1,250-line Barlaam and Josaphat at visually verified ff. 288v-302r and a selective Patrick collation witness at ff. 10r-17r. The catalog and DIMEV Barlaam start f. 228v is a documented locator error.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: second quarter of the fifteenth century

notes: The catalog associates the compiler's modified orthography with Hampshire, eLALME LP 5470; this is not the Barlaam poem's composition place.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript)
entities.description: Direct source of the Barlaam and Josaphat at ff. 100vb-103rc, with f. 102 lost, and Jesus and the Masters at current f. 301rc-vb. Horstmann's Jesus heading says f. 300, superseded by current catalog and image evidence.

physical_description.extent: viii + 342 + i surviving leaves; originally viii + 413
notes: Seventy-one leaves are lost from the original codex, including target f. 102 between the surviving Barlaam passages.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 4196
entities.description: Direct source of the expanded Northern Homily Cycle Barlaam and Josaphat at ff. 199vb-205vb, incomplete at the bottom of the final column.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: fourth quarter of the fourteenth to first quarter of the fifteenth century

physical_description.extent: 258 leaves plus flyleaves, pastedown, and one parchment stub
notes: Current catalog origin: England.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Julius D IX
entities.description: A South English Legendary witness at ff. 36r-44v used selectively for Patrick variants under Horstmann's Ashmole base text; it is not a fourth complete printed stream.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: first half of the fifteenth century

notes: Outer edges were damaged in the 1731 Cotton Library fire.
Current catalog origin: England.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2277
entities.description: A circa-1300 South English Legendary witness discussed by Horstmann as one of the oldest surviving collections. Its opening leaves, including Patrick, are lost, so it supplies no extant target stream or target readings to the edition.

notes: Current catalog origin: South-West England.
The manuscript is imperfect at the beginning; Horstmann reports the first twenty-four legends absent.

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian facsimile of MS Laud Misc. 108
entities.description: The complete published 496-canvas digital object at 1600px, including the Infancy and Patrick target passages.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG images

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian facsimile of MS Ashmole 43
entities.description: The complete published 580-canvas digital object at 1600px, including the Patrick and Conception target passages.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG images

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian facsimile of MS Bodley 779
entities.description: The complete published 672-canvas digital object at 1600px. It verifies Patrick ff. 10r-17r and corrects the Barlaam start from cataloged f. 228v/canvas 470 to f. 288v/canvas 596, ending f. 302r/canvas 623.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG images

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian facsimile of the Vernon Manuscript
entities.description: The complete published 710-canvas digital object at 1600px, representing all surviving manuscript surfaces and directly showing the f. 102 loss and the current f. 301 Jesus passage.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG images

entities.label: British Library catalog descriptions for the four afy7823 witnesses
entities.description: Current Archives and Manuscripts catalog records for Egerton MS 1993, Harley MS 4196, Cotton MS Julius D IX, and Harley MS 2277.

agents.name: Carl Horstmann
notes: Do not conflate this Anglist and editor with the ophthalmologist Carl Wilhelm Horstmann (1847-1912, GND 117531812).

The XML preserves 284 historical TIFF filenames, but no current public image service or complete retrievable bundle was found at the stable-item or collection routes.
Ask U-M Library about the historical 284-TIFF production set, OCR production records, and current reuse terms.

rights.component: Pinned CME submodule XML input, including inherited U-M transcription and markup
rights.basis: The pinned CME root LICENSE contains the Unlicense and repeatedly describes the dedicated subject as software. It does not expressly establish that the repository has authority to dedicate inherited U-M XML, transcription, or markup, so the repository assertion is recorded without treating it as conclusive for this data artifact.
notes: The current U-M item-level No Copyright - United States assertion is recorded separately on the encoding entity.

rights.component: U-M digital text and public delivery
rights.basis: The current U-M item identifies its materials as public domain and applies No Copyright - United States.
notes: NoC-US is jurisdiction-specific and is not itself a license or worldwide public-domain determination.
The legacy XML header says that, where applicable, copyright and other distribution restrictions may apply; that historical caution is preserved rather than erased.

rights.component: Historical U-M TIFF page-image production set for Horstmann's 1875 edition
rights.basis: The current U-M item applies No Copyright - United States and the underlying 1875 edition is public domain in the United States. The historical TIFF set is not currently retrievable, and no separate image-specific license or delivery terms were verified.
notes: NoC-US is jurisdiction-specific and does not establish a worldwide public-domain status or an open license.

rights.component: 1875 editorial text, transcriptions as published, German introduction, notes, and typographical arrangement
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1875, so the applicable United States publication term has expired.

rights.component: Horstmann's introduction, notes, selection, and other original editorial literary contribution
rights.basis: The authoritative identity record establishes Horstmann's birth in 1851 but supplies no death date. Without a verified death date, this review cannot conclusively apply the United Kingdom life-plus-seventy term to his original editorial contribution.
notes: The 1912 death date found for another Carl Horstmann belongs to an ophthalmologist and is not used here.

rights.component: Typographical arrangement of the 1875 published edition
rights.basis: The United Kingdom twenty-five-year term for the typographical arrangement of this 1875 edition has expired, independently of the unresolved death date for Horstmann's literary contribution.

rights.component: Underlying 1875 page content represented in the scan
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1875; the Internet Archive metadata marks the item NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT for the US region.

rights.component: Google scan, OCR, watermarks, and Internet Archive derivative-file presentation
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms / worldwide status not established
rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive metadata supplies no open file license. The embedded Google terms describe the book as public domain for US users but request personal noncommercial use, attribution, retention of the watermark, and avoidance of automated querying; those delivery terms do not change the underlying edition's copyright status.
notes: This record does not treat Google's requested use conditions as copyright in the underlying medieval or 1875 text.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Infancy and Patrick/Purgatory wording, excluding modern catalog description and photography
rights.basis: The medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, and both exact target streams were validly made public in Horstmann's 1875 edition; the UK anonymous-work publication term has expired.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Conception and Patrick wording, excluding modern catalog description and photography
rights.basis: The medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, and both exact target streams were validly made public in Horstmann's 1875 edition; the UK anonymous-work publication term has expired.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Conception, Expanded Nativity, and Patrick wording, excluding modern catalog description and photography
rights.basis: The medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, and the three exact target streams were validly made public in Horstmann's 1875 edition; the UK anonymous-work publication term has expired.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Barlaam and Josaphat wording at ff. 288v-302r, excluding modern catalog description and photography
rights.basis: The medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms and the complete target Barlaam stream was validly made public in Horstmann's 1875 edition; the UK anonymous-work publication term has expired.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Patrick witness wording beyond the selected readings printed by Horstmann
rights.basis: The underlying medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, whether or not Horstmann printed each reading in 1875.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Patrick witness wording beyond the selected readings printed by Horstmann
rights.basis: Horstmann printed selected variants rather than the complete Bodley Patrick stream, and this review did not establish whether all remaining exact wording was lawfully made public before the end of 1988; the UK pre-1989 unpublished-work rule may therefore remain relevant until 31 December 2039.
notes: The specific variants printed in 1875 are public domain; this conservative record concerns the unprinted remainder of the witness stream.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Jesus/Masters and surviving Barlaam wording, excluding modern catalog description and photography
rights.basis: The medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, and both exact surviving target streams were validly made public in Horstmann's 1875 edition; the UK anonymous-work publication term has expired.

rights.component: Underlying surviving medieval Northern Homily Cycle Barlaam wording, excluding modern catalog description and photography
rights.basis: The medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, and the exact surviving incomplete target stream was validly made public in Horstmann's 1875 edition; the UK anonymous-work publication term has expired.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Patrick witness wording beyond the selected readings printed by Horstmann
rights.basis: The underlying medieval wording is outside United States copyright terms, whether or not Horstmann printed each reading in 1875.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Patrick witness wording beyond the selected readings printed by Horstmann
rights.basis: Horstmann printed variants rather than a complete Cotton stream, and this review did not establish complete lawful publication before the end of 1988; the UK pre-1989 unpublished-work rule may remain relevant until 31 December 2039.
notes: The specific variants printed in 1875 are public domain; this conservative record concerns the unprinted remainder of the witness stream.

rights.component: Target Patrick wording formerly carried by lost opening leaves
rights.basis: The target leaves and wording do not survive in the extant manuscript and supplied no target transcription or readings to Horstmann's edition. Rights in other surviving Harley 2277 contents are outside this manifest's target scope.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian photography and metadata
rights.basis: The IIIF attribution and Digital Bodleian terms specify Photo © Bodleian Libraries and CC BY-NC 4.0.

rights.component: Current Bodleian catalog TEI, descriptions, and website presentation
rights.basis: The cached records are modern institutional catalog data; no open license for reusing the TEI or descriptive prose was verified. Digital Bodleian photography has separate CC BY-NC 4.0 records.

rights.component: Current British Library catalog descriptions and website presentation
rights.basis: The cached HTML contains modern institutional catalog descriptions; no open license for wholesale reuse was verified. Any supplied manuscript images and permissions are governed separately through British Library imaging services.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Distinct medieval poems, parallel witness streams, and a selective variant apparatus from eleven direct or collateral manuscript roles
editorial_practices.representation: Six editorial divisions containing eight non-overlapping intellectual works, ten printed witness streams, and selected Cotton and Bodley Patrick readings
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann created the exact 1875 compilation boundary rather than editing one medieval authorial work. He arranged Infancy and Nativity materials, three distinct Barlaam poems, three full Patrick/Purgatory versions, and the Vernon Jesus/Masters debate under six editorial headings.
editorial_practices.scope: The complete 1875 volume and the catalog unit inherited from it

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three complete Patrick/Purgatory witnesses plus collateral Cotton Julius D IX and Bodley 779 witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: Ashmole 43 as the first complete printed stream with Cotton variants and selected Bodley readings, followed by complete Egerton and Laud streams
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann distinguishes full transcription from selective collation: Ashmole, Egerton, and Laud are printed as complete streams, whereas Cotton and the Patrick portion of Bodley contribute only variant readings.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The complete physical 1875 volume, including title page, forty-four pages of German introduction, and printed pp. 1-240
editorial_practices.representation: Edition title page, forty-two empty preliminary PB milestones, p. xliv as a transition, and OCR text of printed pp. 1-240
editorial_practices.description: The U-M Level-1 encoding selects the cataloged title and six text divisions while omitting the verbal content of the introduction; the physical preliminary pages remain traceable through PB milestones.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Physically interleaved edition pages for parallel Ashmole, Egerton, and Laud streams
editorial_practices.representation: A continuous reading-order XML sequence in which PB SEQ records electronic order while PB REF retains the original unique TIFF filename
editorial_practices.description: The encoding serializes each version as a readable sequence. All 284 TIFF references and SEQ values remain unique and consecutive, but REF and SEQ differ at fifty interleaved pages; this is an ordering transformation, not evidence of missing images.
editorial_practices.scope: All 284 PB milestones, especially the Zur Geburt Jesu and Patrick/Purgatory version blocks
notes: Selection is the closest available controlled practice term for this structural serialization; the description preserves the more exact operation.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME, source/CME_phase_1-2/afy7823.xml, pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: HEADER; FRONT/TITLEPAGE; BODY DIV1-DIV2; PB, MILESTONE, LG, and L elements
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the repository artifact, U-M production statement, 1875 source edition, exact encoded text, title, six editorial divisions, version headings, manuscript folio milestones, and linked 284-image production layer.

evidence.citation: Structural and milestone audit of the pinned afy7823.xml repository file.
evidence.locator: 7 DIV1; 7 DIV2; 111 LG; 8,746 L rows; 66 MILESTONE; 284 PB
evidence.summary: Establishes six top-level editorial divisions, seven nested version or appendix divisions, 8,744 verbal verse lines plus two dotted lacuna rows, and 284 unique consecutive PB REF filenames and SEQ values. It also separates Egerton lines 1-276 as DIMEV 4178 from lines 277-1173 as the exact 897-line DIMEV 938 component.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Altenglische legenden, AFY7823.0001.001.
evidence.locator: Item information, rights statement, contents, and stable identifier
evidence.summary: Confirms the 1875 Paderborn item, Carl Horstmann as editor born in 1851, all six encoded divisions, the stable AFY identifier, a public-domain statement, and No Copyright - United States. Direct automated requests encountered a Cloudflare challenge, but the public collection browse route delivered the exact item page during this review.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Contains the Unlicense text and describes the dedicated subject as software; it does not expressly document authority to dedicate inherited U-M transcriptions, OCR, or XML data.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Copyright Policy.
evidence.summary: Explains that item or collection metadata supplies rights information, that users remain responsible for evaluating rights, and that public-domain collection material may be used without permission with requested institutional attribution.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, ed., Altenglische Legenden: Kindheit Jesu; Geburt Jesu; Barlaam und Josaphat; St. Patrik’s Fegefeuer (Paderborn: Ferdinand Schöningh, 1875).
evidence.locator: PDF p. 11 title page; PDF pp. 13-54 introduction; PDF pp. 55-294 printed pp. 1-240
evidence.summary: Complete immediate edition establishing the exact compilation boundary, headings, page ranges, witness claims, transcription streams, selective Patrick apparatus, Vernon lost-leaf estimate, and incomplete Harley ending.

evidence.citation: Horstmann, Altenglische Legenden (1875), introduction and source notes.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 13-54, especially printed pp. xi-xliv; manuscript notes before and within printed pp. 149-240
evidence.summary: Primary editorial-method evidence distinguishing complete base transcriptions from selective variants, naming Cotton and Bodley as Patrick collation witnesses, describing Harley 2277's lost opening, and documenting the Vernon and Harley Barlaam lacunae.

evidence.citation: Page-by-page comparison of afy7823.xml against the complete Horstmann 1875 scan.
evidence.locator: XML PB sequence and edition title page, preliminaries, and printed pp. 1-240
evidence.summary: Confirms that the XML selects the title and six text divisions, represents forty-two preliminary pages as empty PB milestones, retains p. xliv as a transition, and converts physically interleaved parallel-version pages into electronic reading order without deleting the 284 linked image references.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for altenglischeleg00horsgoog.
evidence.locator: identifier, title, creator, date, scanner, contributor, page-progression, rights-region, and external-identifier fields
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1875 edition, Google scanner, Harvard source copy, 304-image extent, OCLC number, ARK, and NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT / US status; it supplies no open license for derivative files.

evidence.citation: Google Book Search usage guidelines embedded in the Internet Archive PDF of Horstmann 1875.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 1-2, English and German usage-guideline leaves
evidence.summary: Calls the book public domain for United States users while requesting personal noncommercial file use, no automated querying, retention of the Google watermark, and independent assessment outside the United States.

evidence.citation: Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek / GND authority record for Carl Horstmann, Anglist and editor, GND 1153444186.
evidence.locator: Name, occupation, birth year and place, external authority identifiers
evidence.summary: Identifies the Altenglische Legenden editor as the Anglist Carl Horstmann, born 1851 in Münster, and supplies no death date. This identity is distinct from the ophthalmologist Carl Wilhelm Horstmann (1847-1912, GND 117531812); the collision prevents use of 1912 in the UK term assessment.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records 2605, 4178, 938, 2965, 4742, 3120, 54, 58, and overlapping record 5875.
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml and linked manuscript and bibliography records; README.md; LICENSE
evidence.summary: Versioned evidence for eight non-overlapping work identities, IMEV/NIMEV identifiers, forms, subjects, direct and collateral witnesses, loci, extents, edition page ranges, the overlapping status of record 5875, and the CC BY 4.0 release license.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Laud Misc. 108.
evidence.locator: Summary, royalItem loci, origin, hands, support, extent, and bibliography
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the current shelfmark, late-thirteenth/early-fourteenth-century core, Hand A, target loci, parchment support, extent, and cautious West Oxfordshire scribal-dialect localization.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Ashmole 43.
evidence.locator: Summary, target contents, origin, date, support, extent, dimensions, and eLALME profile
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the current shelfmark, first-quarter-fourteenth-century date, parchment codex, target loci, dimensions, and Gloucestershire scribal localization identified as eLALME LP 7170.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Bodley 779.
evidence.locator: Summary, target contents, date, origin, support, extent, eLALME profile, and Barlaam locator
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the second-quarter-fifteenth-century paper codex, target Patrick locus, eLALME LP 5470, and the catalog's Barlaam start at f. 228v, preserved as a source error superseded by direct visual evidence for f. 288v.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Eng. poet. a. 1, the Vernon Manuscript.
evidence.locator: Summary, target contents, date, origin, support, extent, dimensions, and lost leaf
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the 1381-1400 parchment codex, West Midlands context, target Jesus/Masters and Barlaam loci, monumental extent, and lost f. 102.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries medieval manuscript catalog records for Laud Misc. 108, Ashmole 43, Bodley 779, and MS Eng. poet. a. 1.
evidence.summary: Aggregate official evidence for current shelfmarks, dates, support, extent, origin and dialect cautions, target loci, the Vernon loss, and the Bodley catalog locator conflict. Exact cached records and checksums are documented by the four individual evidence records.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF object for MS. Laud Misc. 108.
evidence.locator: Complete 496-canvas object; Infancy canvases 31-53; Patrick/Purgatory canvases 204-219
evidence.summary: Complete facsimile evidence for the manuscript and both discontiguous target passages, including exact visual boundaries and Bodleian attribution and license metadata.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF object for MS. Ashmole 43.
evidence.locator: Complete 580-canvas object; Patrick canvases 81-96; Conception canvases 432-439
evidence.summary: Complete facsimile evidence for the manuscript and both discontiguous target passages, including exact visual boundaries and Bodleian attribution and license metadata.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF object for MS. Bodley 779.
evidence.locator: Complete 672-canvas object; Patrick canvases 27-41; Barlaam canvases 596-623; rejected catalog start canvas 470
evidence.summary: Complete facsimile evidence for both target passages and the f. 228v/f. 288v locator conflict, including Bodleian attribution and license metadata.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF object for MS. Eng. poet. a. 1, the Vernon Manuscript.
evidence.locator: Complete 710-canvas object; Barlaam canvases 186-189; Jesus/Masters canvases 488-489
evidence.summary: Complete facsimile evidence for the two target passages, the physical jump over lost f. 102, and the exact Jesus/Masters end before the next work, including Bodleian attribution and license metadata.

evidence.citation: Direct visual inspection of all target ranges in four complete Digital Bodleian IIIF objects.
evidence.summary: Verifies all Bodleian target start and end boundaries, the Vernon f. 102 loss, the Jesus/Masters handoff, and the Bodley Barlaam correction from cataloged f. 228v to visually demonstrated f. 288v. Individual complete-bundle records carry exact checksums.

evidence.citation: Direct comparison of Bodley 779 canvases 470, 596, and 623 with Horstmann's Barlaam incipit, explicit, and XML folio milestones.
evidence.locator: canvas 470 = f. 228v unrelated text; canvas 596 = f. 288v exact incipit; canvas 623 = f. 302r exact explicit
evidence.summary: Demonstrates that the operational Barlaam locus is ff. 288v-302r, not the f. 228v start repeated by the current catalog and DIMEV.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Egerton MS 1993.
evidence.locator: Title, date, support, format, scope and content description
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the current shelfmark, fourteenth-century vellum manuscript, small-folio format, and broad content; Horstmann and DIMEV supply exact target loci because the holding record is coarse.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 4196.
evidence.locator: Title, date, physical description, contents, and access
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the current shelfmark, late-fourteenth/early-fifteenth-century parchment codex, physical extent and damage, and absence of a current complete public digital object.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Julius D IX.
evidence.locator: Title, date, physical description, contents, access, and digital-content status
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the current shelfmark, medieval parchment codex, physical description, and unavailable legacy digital object; Horstmann and DIMEV supply the exact Patrick collation locus.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 2277.
evidence.locator: Title, date, physical description, imperfection at the beginning, contents, and access
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the current shelfmark, medieval parchment codex, lost opening leaves, and unavailable legacy digital object. The target Patrick component no longer survives and contributes no printed reading.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts records for Egerton MS 1993, Harley MS 4196, Cotton MS Julius D IX, and Harley MS 2277.
evidence.summary: Aggregate official evidence for current shelfmarks, broad manuscript dates, physical descriptions, collection contents, damage or incompleteness, and the lack of currently delivered complete public images. Individual cached records carry exact checksums.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, Terms of use.
evidence.summary: States that most Digital Bodleian images are offered under CC BY-NC 4.0 with attribution, preservation of notices, noncommercial use, and responsibility for any additional restrictions; the four target IIIF objects carry matching attribution metadata.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: Explains requestable imaging, fees, user responsibility for third-party rights, and the requirement for written permission before reproducing supplied files.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Official United States guidance on copyright duration, supporting the expired publication term of the 1875 edition and the public-domain status of medieval wording in the United States.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office, Copyright notice: duration of copyright (term).
evidence.summary: Official United Kingdom guidance on life-plus-seventy literary-work terms, anonymous works, typographical arrangements, and special pre-1989 unpublished-work rules used for the jurisdiction-specific assessments and unresolved cases here.

open_questions.question: Can U-M supply the historical 284 TIFF production files, OCR workflow records, and current reuse terms for AFY7823.0001.001?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for the complete TIFF production set and any file manifest.
Request documentation of OCR, image-to-PB linking, and the REF-to-SEQ reading-order transformation.
Record any supplied files and contract terms as a new access layer without replacing the extant repository XML.

open_questions.question: Can complete current color facsimiles be obtained for Egerton MS 1993, Harley MS 4196, and Cotton MS Julius D IX?
next_steps: Ask British Library Imaging Services to check for existing complete color files before commissioning new photography.
Identify ff. 27-40 and 119v-128r in Egerton, ff. 199vb-205vb in Harley 4196, and ff. 36r-44v in Cotton as target ranges while requesting complete-codex coverage separately.
Obtain written reuse and repository-redistribution terms before retaining or publishing supplied files.

open_questions.question: Will the Bodleian catalog and DIMEV correct the Bodley 779 Barlaam start from f. 228v to the visually demonstrated f. 288v?
next_steps: Send the providers canvas 470, canvas 596, and canvas 623 with the exact incipit, explicit, and Horstmann folio milestones.
Preserve both the claimed and corrected locators with provenance until provider records change.

open_questions.question: Which exact Cotton Julius D IX and Bodley 779 Patrick readings did Horstmann collate, and can every apparatus note be assigned to a current folio and line?
next_steps: Transcribe Horstmann's Patrick apparatus into a source-indexed collation table.
Use the complete Bodley facsimile and newly obtained Cotton images to verify each reading.
Continue modeling Cotton and Bodley as selective collation witnesses, not complete printed streams.

open_questions.question: Can the approximately 480 verses lost with Vernon f. 102 be reconstructed from an independent copy of DIMEV 54?
next_steps: Monitor DIMEV and new manuscript identifications for another witness.
Keep Horstmann's dotted lacuna and resumed lineation explicit rather than silently joining f. 101v to f. 103r.

open_questions.question: Does the CME repository's Unlicense assertion validly cover the inherited U-M OCR, transcription, and XML markup in afy7823.xml?
next_steps: Ask the CME maintainer to state the intended license scope and provenance of authority for inherited corpus files.
Retain U-M item-level rights and the underlying 1875 text assessment separately from repository licensing.
Do not treat the software-oriented license wording as conclusive permission for all data layers without clarification.

open_questions.question: Can an authoritative source establish the death date of the Anglist and editor Carl Horstmann, born 1851, without conflating him with the ophthalmologist Carl Wilhelm Horstmann?
next_steps: Query GND, VIAF, NACO, IdRef, and archival or university personnel records under exact authority identifiers.
Reject the ophthalmologist's 1847-1912 dates and GND 117531812 as an identity collision.
Reassess the UK literary and editorial copyright term only after a death date is supported.

open_questions.question: Was all exact Cotton Julius D IX and Bodley 779 Patrick wording lawfully made public before the end of 1988, or can the UK pre-1989 unpublished-work term still affect unprinted witness text?
next_steps: Identify later complete diplomatic editions or other lawful publications of both witness streams.
Separate variants printed by Horstmann in 1875 from the unprinted remainder.
Seek specialist UK copyright advice before publishing a new complete transcription of unprinted witness wording.
